content:
I've worked with Blackboard, Canvas, and the Google Suite the duration of my undergraduate and graduate degree . I whole-heartedly embrace the use of these platforms in the classroom and at home, but more importantly, I have found in my class the students do as well. My classes are entirely scheduled and planned before the first day of the semester begins, allowing for students to understand the full depth of the work before them right from the get-go. I believe that any intellectual endeavor should be approached like working any other muscle: consistent practice, multiple times a week. This is why when I've taught classes online, I still had students submit work multiple times per week and engage with each other's work in a sort of virtual classroom for an attendance grade. I would increase student success by sending out assignment reminders, consistently grading assignments, and one-to-one Skype sessions.




I'm proficient in web design, audio/visual production and editing, and the Adobe Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, etc.). Basically, I'm the person everyone in my circle comes to when they have a technology related question.
context: From document: Student Success Statement — chunk 2 (chunk 2/11)
